items.title: Preferred bidder announced for troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter

children.content: A consortium headed by Adroit Capital is announced as the preferred bidder for the smelter in northern Tasmania, as federal and state government pledge another $5 million to support workers in the weeks ahead.

gallery.altText: A large smelter looking dark and deserted.

thumbnail.altText: A large smelter looking dark and deserted.

items.title: Maugean skates released after more than two years in captivity

children.content: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.

image.altText: A man holds a Maugean skate in the water

items.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops

children.content: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.

image.altText: A seastar at Hallett Cove.

items.title: Tasmania's Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary to star in new docuseries

children.content: It's not all cute and fluffy animal stories at Bonorong. The wildlife sanctuary's director Greg Irons says a new docuseries will show the real face of animal conservation, and it's not all "happy Larry".

gallery.altText: A man holding a wombat and being filmed by a camera man
Man smiles for photo in front of shed that says 'BONORONG'
Two women looking at a computer screen and chatting

thumbnail.altText: A man holding a wombat and being filmed by a camera man
Man smiles for photo in front of shed that says 'BONORONG'
Two women looking at a computer screen and chatting

avatar.alt: A woman with long, brown hair in  pink jumper.

items.title: Tasmania 'just wanted to roll trucks in' as quick fix for icebreaker refuelling saga

children.content: A federal department says the Tasmanian government's proposed interim solution to a costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker is "far from" acceptable.

image.altText: The red-hulled Nuyina next to the dock in Hobart.

items.title: Spirit ferries operator set for major restructure under Tasmanian Labor plan

children.content: The two government businesses responsible for the botched Spirit of Tasmania ship replacement program would undergo a major restructure, with one stripped of all assets, under a Tasmanian Labor government.

image.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

items.title: Youth escapee avoids recording conviction for knifepoint carjacking of tourists

children.content: One of the teenagers involved in a group escape from the Ashley Youth Detention Centre in December is sentenced for his involvement in the armed robbery of two tourists.

image.altText: A coastal town along a beach with yellow sand under a partly cloudy sky seen from above.

items.title: 'Intolerable': When $786m bridge was being designed, engineers raised a red flag

children.content: Despite government assurances Hobart's Bridgewater Bridge is safe, there are fresh concerns over its foundation design, with one expert who worked on the project suggesting it could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

image.altText: A silver metal device attached to a concrete pylon of a bridge.

items.title: This 24yo started gambling as a teenager and has now lost 900k

children.content: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teenagers gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

gallery.altText: A boy leaning against a brick wall looks down at his phone.
Boy wearing a hoodie looks at his phone.
A boy with bruised knuckles sits and looks at his mobile phone.

thumbnail.altText: A boy leaning against a brick wall looks down at his phone.
Boy wearing a hoodie looks at his phone.
A boy with bruised knuckles sits and looks at his mobile phone.

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight

children.content: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of yesterday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

image.altText: A Qatar plane on the tarmac at Melbourne international airport.

items.title: Bystanders foil alleged kidnap attempt of woman changing tyre in Sydney

children.content: A 27-year-old woman was changing a tyre when a man allegedly threatened her with a knife and attempted to drag her into an SUV, NSW Police said.

image.altText: A man in a tracksuit is handcuffed while surrounded by several NSW Police officers.

items.title: NASA has revealed its plan to live on the Moon. Here's how it would work

children.content: The US space agency has outlined plans for a sprawling lunar outpost involving rovers, hopping drones and resource-extraction technology as part of a renewed race to the Moon.

gallery.altText: An artist's rendering showing astronauts, habitats, rovers, power systems and cargo operations at the Moon Base.
Artist’s rendering depicting lunar surface operations at a future base in the lunar South Pole.
Artist’s rendering of the lunar South Pole region

thumbnail.altText: An artist's rendering showing astronauts, habitats, rovers, power systems and cargo operations at the Moon Base.
Artist’s rendering depicting lunar surface operations at a future base in the lunar South Pole.
Artist’s rendering of the lunar South Pole region

items.title: Russia looks for new soldiers in Transnistria as war casualty numbers grow

children.content: The Kremlin is trying to recruit more soldiers for its war in Ukraine but an analyst says at the rate of casualties, "Russia cannot replace recruits fast enough to keep the 'meat grinder' fed."

image.altText: Russian President Vladimir Putin  reaches his hand across a table

items.title: Pentagon denies reports of clash with SpaceX over Starlink price hike

children.content: The Pentagon has denied claims that the department has been clashing with Elon Musk's SpaceX over costs to use its satellite internet network, which the military's kamikaze drones rely upon in the war against Iran.

image.altText: SpaceX founder Elon Musk speaks at a press conference

items.title: Record-breaking rain welcomed in drought-hit parts of NSW

children.content: Up to 100 millimetres of rain has been welcomed across drought-hit parts of western NSW, but some farmers expecting relief were left disappointed.

image.altText: Wet paddocks through the window of a ute driving through the landscape

gallery.altText: Steady rain over brown paddocks with water starting to pool.

thumbnail.altText: Steady rain over brown paddocks with water starting to pool.

items.title: ABC's Justin Stevens resigns as director of news

children.content: Justin Stevens, the ABC's director of news, has announced his resignation after 19 years with the organisation.

image.altText: Headshot of Justin Stevens

items.title: NSW concern about cost of monitoring IS-linked women and children — as it happened

children.content: The boss of home affairs says the NSW government raised concerns about the cost of monitoring IS-linked women and children who recently returned to the country from Syria.

image.altText: Stephanie Foster PSM

items.title: Australian travellers urged to check measles immunity

children.content: Authorities keep a close eye on measles outbreaks in Asia, as many cases reported in Australia continue to be returned overseas travellers.

image.altText: A vial of the measles, mumps, and rubella (MMR) vaccine is pictured

items.title: Cuts of meat that will give you more bang for your buck

children.content: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

gallery.altText: Two women looking at a packet of pre-packaged meat at a supermarket
A large piece of steak on a grill, sliced into smaller pieces
A black bowl of lamb curry in gravy sits on top of a wooden chopping board on a bench.

thumbnail.altText: Two women looking at a packet of pre-packaged meat at a supermarket
A large piece of steak on a grill, sliced into smaller pieces
A black bowl of lamb curry in gravy sits on top of a wooden chopping board on a bench.

avatar.alt: Portrait of journalist Daniel Johnson wearing a grey and maroon plaid shirt and crossing his arms.

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

image.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: More or Less quiz: We need you to rank five State of Origin greats

children.content: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.title: More than a million worshippers brave searing heat for annual Hajj pilgrimage

children.content: Thousands of Muslim worshippers converge on the rocky hills and plains of Saudi Arabia's Mount Arafat as the annual Islamic pilgrimage, the Hajj, reached its peak.

gallery.altText: men pray against a rising sun
men in white robes stand on top of a hill against a rising sun horizon

thumbnail.altText: men pray against a rising sun
men in white robes stand on top of a hill against a rising sun horizon

items.title: Police search for missing swimmer in WA's South West

children.content: Police say the woman entered the water at Koombana Bay just after 9:20am and has not been seen since

image.altText: Police standing at the shoreline of Koombana Bay

items.title: Court increases jail sentence for remorseless sex worker killer

children.content: Xiaozheng Lin will spend more time in prison for killing two sex workers in 2022, following an appeal by the prosecutor.

image.altText: Xiaozheng Lin stands against a grey backdrop in a police mug shot.

items.title: Council buys land for $1, but what's the catch?

children.content: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

image.altText: A drone image of a block of land with bushland on surrounded by buildings, road and the beach.

items.title: Teaching student charged with online child abuse offences

children.content: A Victorian teaching student, who had recently worked as a childcare centre cook, a foster carer
and was a school council president, is facing charges relating to online child abuse material.

image.altText: A man with glasses smiles at the camera.

items.title: Heavy rain on Gus Lamont's family station prompts police to renew search

children.content: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

items.title: Headline inflation eases to 4.2 per cent in April as fuel prices fall

children.content: Headline inflation decelerated in April, with consumer prices increasing at an annual pace of 4.2 per cent, down from 4.6 per cent in March.

image.altText: photo of a hand with empty cup of coffee and cash on a wooden table

items.title: Media leaks ahead of Ben Roberts-Smith arrest referred to corruption commission

children.content: News crews were at Sydney Airport as a plane carrying Ben Roberts-Smith landed, and captured images of his arrest from inside the terminal.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith, face blurred, exits plane onto Sydney Airport tarmac flanked by officers and men in suits.

items.title: Original track designer steers clear of estimated $100m MotoGP remodel

children.content: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

image.altText: A narrow bitumen road runs between trees and large patches of grass

items.title: Woman forced out of home as mould grows after insurer repairs roof

children.content: Lily lodged an insurance claim with her provider GIO Suncorp after a truck ran through her street's power lines. Nearly a decade later, she has been forced out of her home.

image.altText: Lily looks at the camera in her garden wearing a red shirt

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

gallery.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

thumbnail.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

items.title: Australian students record worst tech literacy results on record

children.content: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

image.altText: A pair of unidentifiable hands type on a laptop, with other people on laptops in the background.

items.title: Man to stand trial for alleged murder of woman found in Cobram home

children.content: A man charged over the murder of a woman who was found dead in a Northern Victorian home has been committed to stand trial over her death.

image.altText: A man standing in a shopping centre posing for a photo

items.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet slowly restored

children.content: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

image.altText: Smoke billows across a city.
